rss.title: A hidden pollutant is changing how the world's forests breathe
rss.snippet: A massive global analysis found that nitrogen pollution can either speed up or dramatically slow the natural "breathing" of forest soils, depending on the ecosystem's condition. The results reveal hidden tipping points that could affect how forests store carbon and cope with climate change.

rss.title: This newly discovered raptor may have hunted like a giant heron
rss.snippet: A newly discovered raptor-like dinosaur from Patagonia is changing how scientists think about ancient predators. rss.title: Scientists say evolution may work differently than we thought
rss.snippet: A major research study is challenging one of evolution’s most influential ideas: that most genetic changes that become permanent are essentially neutral. arxiv_papers.title: An information-geometric framework for mapping maximum potential biodiversity
arxiv_papers.snippet: Biodiversity measures are often used descriptively: one computes a diversity index from an observed or estimated community composition and maps the resulting values across space. arxiv_papers.title: Disentangling the interactive effects of anthropogenic disturbances on biodiversity
arxiv_papers.snippet: Anthropogenic activity threatens biodiversity through climate change, habitat fragmentation, and increasing frequency and scale of disturbance.
